rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
generic_rules: Maintain existing component type and implementation scopes during fusion
Preserve all existing subcomponents and connections when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Do not invent new declarations; use only visible identifiers from the current component scope
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not invent new declarations; use only visible identifiers from the current component scope
irrelevant_topics: compact requirement semantics and model traceability
valid AGREE syntax, visible-symbol usage, and useful contract patterns
